commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared UI session-key ownership helper, filters dashboard sidebar Recent sessions to the selected agent while preserving legacy default-agent sessions, and adds render regression tests.

PR surface: Source +28, Tests +106. Total +134 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main's sidebar Recent list has a clear source-level reproduction path with mixed Main and Work session rows because it filters session type but not agent ownership; the PR adds a render test for that state.
commands.review_followup: - No ClawSweeper repair lane is needed; the branch already contains the focused code and test changes, leaving normal maintainer review and required checks.

target.title: fix #88214: [Feature]: Dashboard sidebar Recent sessions should filter by currently selected agent
target.body:
## Summary

- Behavior or issue addressed: dashboard sidebar Recent sessions no longer mixes sessions from other agents when an agent-scoped chat is active.
- Filter the dashboard sidebar Recent session list to the currently selected agent.
- Keep legacy non-agent session keys visible only for the default agent, while preserving existing exclusions for archived, cron, global, unknown, subagent, and spawned sessions.
- Add a render-level regression test that mixes Main and Work agent sessions and verifies the sidebar only lists the active Work agent sessions.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: dashboard sidebar Recent sessions no longer mixes sessions from other agents when an agent-scoped chat is active.
- Real environment tested: headless Google Chrome driven by Playwright, with a Vite proof page loading the real `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` from this worktree and rendering mixed Main/Work sessions.
- Evidence after fix: terminal capture from `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88214-evidence/sidebar-recent-agent-filter-browser.log`, JSON output at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88214-evidence/sidebar-recent-agent-filter-browser.json`, and screenshot generated at `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88214-evidence/sidebar-recent-agent-filter-browser.png`.
- Target test file: `ui/src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88214-evidence/capture-sidebar-proof.mjs`
- Observed result after fix: Chromium rendered the sidebar Recent list with labels `Work new` and `Work older`; `Main old` was absent from sidebar Recent labels.
- What was not tested: a full gateway-backed manual app session; the browser proof uses a Vite harness with real `renderApp` and synthetic session rows to isolate the sidebar Recent behavior.

```json
{
  "labels": [
    "Work new",
    "Work older"
  ],
  "mainOldVisible": 0,
  "passed": true
}
```

## Regression Test Plan

- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-88214 tsgo:test:ui` and `cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-88214/ui && pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts --project unit src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`.
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm --dir /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/issue-88214 tsgo:test:ui`
  - Evidence: `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88214-evidence/ui-tsgo-test-ui.log`
- `cd ui && pnpm exec vitest run --config vitest.config.ts --project unit src/ui/app-render.assistant-avatar.test.ts`
  - Evidence: `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88214-evidence/ui-vitest-app-render-sidebar-targeted.log`
- `node /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88214-evidence/capture-sidebar-proof.mjs`
  - Evidence: `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-issue-88214-evidence/sidebar-recent-agent-filter-browser.log`
- What was not tested: a full gateway-backed manual app session; no external service or user data was required for this isolated sidebar rendering proof.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: the dashboard sidebar Recent list only filtered out non-user-facing session types and then sorted globally, so agent-scoped dashboard sessions from other agents remained eligible for the active agent's sidebar.
- The top-level chat/session state already carries the selected agent through agent-prefixed session keys and agent selection state; the sidebar list needed to apply the same selected-agent boundary before slicing recent sessions.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
